framing: Corporate solicitor at magic circle firm seeking move to in-house counsel at fintech

jd.title: Senior Legal Counsel — Commercial & Product

requirements.text: 6+ years PQE in commercial, technology, or financial services law
Experience drafting and negotiating SaaS/platform agreements, API licensing, and data processing agreements
Knowledge of UK/EU financial services regulation (FCA, PSD2, EMD2)
Experience advising product teams on regulatory implications of new features
Familiarity with UK/EU data protection law (UK GDPR, EU GDPR, ePrivacy)
Experience working in-house at a technology or fintech company
Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) or equivalent jurisdiction
Experience with open banking or payments infrastructure
Ability to operate autonomously in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

responsibilities: Draft and negotiate commercial agreements with enterprise clients, banking partners, and technology vendors
Advise product and engineering teams on regulatory compliance for new financial products across UK and EU markets
Manage outside counsel relationships and litigation matters
Develop scalable legal playbooks and contract templates to support high-volume deal flow
Support regulatory licensing applications and ongoing compliance with FCA requirements

expected_output.summary: Corporate solicitor with 7 years PQE at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer, specialising in technology and financial services transactions. Led legal workstreams on 15+ fintech M&A and investment deals (combined value GBP 2.1B). Deep knowledge of FCA regulatory framework, PSD2, and UK GDPR. Experienced in SaaS licensing, data processing agreements, and cross-border commercial contracts. Seeking transition to in-house counsel role at a high-growth fintech.

experience.title: Senior Associate — Technology & Financial Services
bullets: Led legal due diligence and drafting on 15+ fintech M&A and investment transactions (combined value GBP 2.1B)
Drafted and negotiated SaaS licensing agreements, API access agreements, and data processing addenda for financial technology clients
Advised e-money institutions and payment service providers on FCA authorisation, PSD2 compliance, and safeguarding requirements
Developed firm's template GDPR data processing agreement used across 50+ client engagements
Seconded to a major UK digital bank (6 months, 2022) supporting commercial contracts and product compliance

experience.title: Associate — Corporate & Commercial
bullets: Supported partners on cross-border M&A transactions in technology and financial services sectors
Drafted commercial agreements including software licensing, reseller agreements, and service contracts
Assisted with regulatory filings for FCA-authorised clients including variation of permissions applications
Trained 3 trainee solicitors on commercial contract drafting and negotiation

certifications: GDPR Practitioner Certificate — International Association of Privacy Professionals (IAPP CIPP/E)
education: LLB Law (First Class Honours) — University of Bristol (2012-2015)

jd_requirements: 6+ years PQE commercial technology financial services law
SaaS platform agreements API licensing data processing agreements
UK EU financial services regulation FCA PSD2 EMD2
advising product teams regulatory implications new features
UK EU data protection GDPR ePrivacy
deliberate_flaws: No in-house experience (entire career at one law firm) — JD prefers in-house
Only 6-month secondment at a digital bank, not sustained in-house work
No direct payments infrastructure experience (advised PSPs but didn't build payment products)
Magic circle culture is very different from startup/scaleup pace — culture fit risk
No experience with scalable legal operations or legal tech tooling (CLM platforms, etc.)

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
